profile_bio.description: Advisor to startups. Freelancer. @LeadershipData founder. Global Speaker. Top #B2B influencer & social promoter #DataScience #AI #ML. PhD Astrophysics @Caltech

kept.text:
Get "Mathematics of Machine Learning" here: https://t.co/07exFk5LqL by @TivadarDanka v/ @PacktDataML 
—
GitHub: https://t.co/2ENjzhr35C
—
Here is my review:

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗦𝗲𝘁 𝗢𝗳 𝗠𝗮𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗰𝗮𝗹 𝗔𝗹𝗴𝗼𝗿𝗶𝘁𝗵𝗺𝘀 𝗧𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗟𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗻 𝗙𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝗘𝘅𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲

This massive book is incredible, with its comprehensive coverage of numerous fields of mathematics and their intersection with the world of AI, data science, and machine learning (AI+DSML). I remember the very first time that I encountered machine learning. This was 20+ years ago, and that was already after 20+ years of being drenched in advanced mathematics as an astrophysicist. 

That first encounter of mine with ML was this definition: "Machine learning is the set of mathematical algorithms that learn from experience" (slightly paraphrased from the original quote by Tom Mitchell, CMU). That definition surprised me, confused me, motivated me, and changed the course of my career from astrophysics into AI+DSML. 

This book by Tivadar Danka captures the full meaning of that definition. The book covers thoroughly the many areas and domains of mathematics through which patterns in data are detected, described, learned, and recognized - all for the benefit of powering ML and AI algorithms, applications, and aspirations. 

This book will motivate you, surprise you, and inspire you in many ways, no matter what level of mathematics has (or has not) already propelled your career journey. There is room for all of us to grow. 

This is a great book, worthy to sit on everyone's desktop, ready to help you explore and exploit the full set of mathematical algorithms that learn from experience. 

The book is accompanied by a rich GitHub code repository of Jupyter notebooks. Learn by doing! Do by learning!

Disclosure: the publisher provided me with a free review copy of the book.

$AMZN $FDX $UPS
Amazon’dan Lojistik Dünyasında "AWS" Devrimi: FedEx ve UPS Hisseleri Düştü

Amazon, lojistik ağını kendi satıcı ekosisteminin dışındaki tüm işletmelere açtığını duyurarak "Amazon Tedarik Zinciri Hizmetleri" (ASCS) platformunu resmen başlattı. 

Bu hamleyle Amazon; 100 uçak, 80.000 tır ve 24.000 konteynerden oluşan devasa altyapısını Procter & Gamble ve 3M gibi dünya devlerinin kullanımına sunarak doğrudan FedEx ve UPS’in pazar payını hedefledi.

Haberin ardından lojistik sektöründe "tedarik zinciri şoku" yaşanırken, FedEx hisseleri %2,5, UPS hisseleri ise %1,8 oranında değer kaybetti. Amazon'un hammadde taşımacılığından son kullanıcı teslimatına kadar uçtan uca sunduğu bu yeni hizmet, analistler tarafından "lojistiğin AWS'si" olarak nitelendirildi. Geleneksel kargo devleri için ciddi bir rekabet tehdidi oluşturan bu dijitalleşme hamlesi, küresel navlun ve paket teslimatı pazarındaki dengeleri Amazon lehine değiştirdi.

tweet.text: Wprost z wydatkami CAPEX-owymi Microsoftu $MSFT, Amazona $AMZN i Alphabetu $GOOGL, o których pisałem w poprzednim wpisie powiązany jest backlog określany też jako RPO (Remaining Performance Obligations). RPO mają to do siebie, że są pewnym zwiastunem przyszłych przychodów, jakie rozpozna spółka, ale jeśli podmiotowi, który zobowiązał się do nabycia usług/produktów innej firmy coś nie wyjdzie to wtedy backlog wyparowuje i nie przekształca się w przychody (często, w przypadku zobowiązań rozłożonych na lata, automatycznie spadają też wówczas nakłady na CapEx). Powodem, dla którego $MSFT obecnie nie zyskuje uznania w oczach inwestorów są nie tylko zagrożenia SaaSowe, ale właśnie to, że ponad 40% RPO stanowią zobowiązania pochodzące od OpenAI, z którym Microsoft ma burzliwą relację i gdzie nie ma gwarancji, że OpenAI uzyska finansowanie na pokrycie wszystkich swoich zobowiązań (nie tylko wobec Microsoftu, ale także wobec Oracle czy Amazonu). Nie zmienia to jednak faktu, że nawet gdyby z góry uznać, że OpenAI nie wywiąże się z żadnego zobowiązania to dalej RPO Microsoftu na tle konkurencji do niedawna było wyraźnie wyższe. Fakt, że obecnie Alphabet czy Amazon mają zdrowsze RPO (nie obciążone w tak mocnym stopniu zobowiązaniami chwiejnej firmy Sama Altmana) sprawia, że Microsoft na ich tle wygląda bardziej ryzykownie.

tweet.text:
$AMD| Analysts forecasts are all wrong 🧵

Even UBS at $455, they modeled AMD at 
$49B FY2026
$75B FY2027
$95B FY2028

Most other analysts are at $40-$45B for FY2026. 
They are still excluding $MSFT $META @OpenAI $ORCL @LumaLabsAI $AMZN $GOOGL XAI G42 and many more. The demand for EPYC Turin and Venice are off the chart, estimated to be more than 20m units due to explosive Agentic AI demand. 

Dr. Su secured the 2nm allocation, Memory and customers, yet...

We know so far Dr. Su said Helios rack is on track to deliver in H2 2026, so 2-2.5GW is easily 
$40-$50B at conseravtive point. 

When we exclude $40-$50B, which is nonsense
$AMD will still going to do $40-$45B, could be higher if AMD could secure more EPYC Turin and Venice. 

Like I said, most analysts are still using old and false new "Delay" from SemiAnal to model AMD. 

Months ago, before @OpenAI raised $122B+, they would pound on OpenAI can't fund the first GW. Now OpenAI has roughly $170-$200B Cash, yet they still excluded OpenAI revenue out. 

On $META, it has $81.2B cash and $115.8 Operating Cash Flow, so for fuck sake, META can fund multi-GW deployment. 

AMD has the worst coverages than all other semi stocks for more than a decade. They have no idea how to understand TSMC 2nm Ramp roadmap. I will link it below. 

I understand AMD only guide quarter by quarter, but contracts are already announced, analysts don't have to be this stubborn until last minute. 

Now you know what I been calling them out.

Not Financial Advice!

quoted_tweet.text:
$AMD Comprehensive Q1 2026 Earning Preview🧵

@AMD  is set to release its fiscal Q1 2026 results on May 5, 2026 after market close. AMD guides Quarter by quarter, so the inflection Q3 will be guided in Q2 2026 ER or August.

AMD Guidance: $9.8B (±$300 million)

Wall Street Consensus:
Revenue $9.8-$9.88B 
Adjusted EPS $1.28-$1.30

My Expectation:
Revenue $10.3-$11B 
Adjusted EPS $1.30-$1.40

Analysts Price Targets so far:
UBS $455 | Buy Rating
Melius Research $375
DA Davidson $375| Buy Rating
Sussquehanna $375| Positive 
Wells Fargo $345| Overweight

Investors/Analysts will watch:
~H2 2026 Helios Shipment and ramp 
~Explosive Agentic AI demand for EPYC Turin and Venice
~CPUs demand for older gen as reported that customers demand is so high they are buying 2022-2023 EPYC Gen
~Potential Large customer announcement
~China potential revenue or progress on export licenses
~TSMC allocation and supply management
~CPU:GPU Ratio shifted from 1:4-8 to 3-5:1. Management comment on this whether CPUs dense custom racks are being placed. 
~Custom Chips progress like $MSFT HBv 
~Progress on "Local AI" chips for B2C Laptop/PC

“Agentic AI” (autonomous AI agents capable of multi-step reasoning, planning, and tool use) is emerging for AMD as it has the best CPUs(Turin) and EPYC Venice will widen the innovative Gap even further. Beyond traditional generative AI training/inference. Unlike pure LLM inference (GPU-heavy), agentic workloads require significant CPU orchestration for sequential decision-making, tool calling, and hybrid compute. AMD’s strong position in both EPYC server CPUs and high-memory Instinct GPUs (MI300/MI350/MI455 series) positions it well to gorw EPS(Non-GAAP) to $12-$17.

Not Financial Advice!

article.title: Introducing Amazon Supply Chain Services: Amazon’s logistics network, now open to every business
article.preview_text:
Any business can now move, store, and deliver everything from raw materials to finished products using the same supply chain that supports Amazon and its independent selling partners.
Key takeaways
